title: Dex / dexDRONES Open Cultural Infrastructure
thesis_claim: dexDRONES extends Dex as operational public infrastructure by pairing open licensing with production-grade field capture and metadata discipline.
cybernetic_loop: Field capture publishes to commons, reuse metrics and partner demand feed back into commissioning and infrastructure decisions, reshaping future capture cycles.
material_system: Dex operations CLI, site/data manifests, press artifacts, and nonprofit governance workflows.
agency_model: Institutional cybernetics: public reuse behavior recursively informs organizational production strategy.

evidence_refs.label: Dex Manual
evidence_refs.excerpt: The Dex operations manual defines canonical command centers for entry, content, and infrastructure workflows.

evidence_refs.label: About Data
evidence_refs.excerpt: Dex commissions and publishes artist-made audiovisual work for legal reuse under CC BY 4.0 with rights-forward production design.

evidence_refs.label: dexDRONES Launch
evidence_refs.excerpt: dexDRONES launched as Dex's aerial wing focused on film-quality CC-BY 4.0 assets for public reuse.

risk_notes: Open-media rhetoric can appear abstract unless paired with concrete workflow and metrics evidence.
Press-style narration can overshadow cybernetic analysis without explicit feedback-loop framing.
